title: DIY Trunk Side Table
summary: A slat-paneled trunk on bun feet — four framed 1x4 panels around a plywood base, with a mitered lid that opens for storage.
description:
This is a faux-vintage steamer trunk built as real storage furniture: four wall panels, each a row of horizontal 1x4 slats captured inside a simple 1x2 frame, screwed to a plywood base and topped with a mitered lid. Building each panel as its own framed unit — rather than slat by slat directly onto the trunk — is what makes the slats sit flush and even; fit five boards into a frame on a flat bench and they line up far more easily than five boards fastened one at a time to a standing box.

The two-tone finish (a wood-tone stain on the slats, white paint on the frames, or whatever combination you like) is what sells the vintage-trunk look, so mask carefully between the two before finishing. Decorative nail heads along the base plywood and real hinges plus a support arm or chain for the lid are what make it function and read as a trunk rather than just a slatted box with a flat top.

tools.note: Mitering the lid frame at 45° is the one precision cut in this build.

cutList.note: Five slats per panel, front and back.
Five slats per panel, both sides.
Fill the mitered lid frame's center opening — scrap pieces cut to fit work as well as a single panel.

steps.title: Build the front and back panels
steps.body: Glue and screw (or pocket-screw) the five 1x4 slats together edge to edge to form each panel face. Attach the 1x2 frame verticals to the panel's outside edges first, then attach the bottom horizontal — leave the top horizontal off for now, you'll add it after the panel is standing.

steps.title: Build the side panels
steps.body: Build the two side panels the same way, gluing and nailing the bottom horizontal on with a nail gun and glue since the 1-1/4" pocket screws used elsewhere are too long for this thinner joint here — a smaller pocket screw works too if you have one.

steps.title: Join the four panels and attach the base
steps.body: Join the four panels at the corners into a box, then add the remaining top horizontal 1x2 pieces now that the box is standing. Attach the assembled box to the 1/4" plywood base with screws, nails, or glue.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Install the four bun feet per the hardware manufacturer's instructions.

steps.title: Build the lid
steps.body: Miter the four lid frame pieces at 45° and join them into a frame with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Fill the center opening with scrap pieces cut to fit, or a single panel cut to size. Screw the support cleats to the underside of the frame.

steps.title: Finish and add hardware
steps.body: Sand everything, then finish with your chosen two-tone combination — for example, a wood-tone stain on the slats and white paint on the frames, masking carefully between the two. Tack decorative nail heads along the base if desired. Attach the lid with hinges and a support arm or chain so it stays open on its own.

images.alt: Slat-paneled trunk side table with bun feet and a hinged lid
